title: Washington Place (West Virginia)
entities_description: The Emancipation Proclamation of 1863 allowed for the construction of Washington Place, one of the first homes built by freed slaves in West Virginia.
evidence_sentences: Washington Place ( William Washington House ) is one of the first homes built by freed slaves after the Emancipation Proclamation of 1863 in Hampshire County , West Virginia , United States .
Washington Place was built by William and Annie Washington in north Romney between 1863 and 1874 on land given to Annie by her former owner , Susan Blue Parsons of Wappocomo plantation .
One of his subdivisions is the " Blacks Hill " neighborhood of Romney , adjacent to the Washington Place homestead .

title: IBM Research – Brazil
entities_description: IBM Research – Brazil is one of twelve research laboratories comprising IBM Research.
evidence_sentences: IBM Research – Brazil is one of twelve research laboratories comprising IBM Research , its first in South America .

Engineer and associate lab director Ulisses Mello explains that IBM has four priority areas in Brazil : " The main area is related to natural resources management , involving oil and gas , mining and agricultural sectors .
In collaboration with Brazil 's government , it will help IBM to develop technology systems around natural resource development and large - scale events such as the 2016 Summer Olympics .

title: Lookin Ass
entities_description: "Lookin Ass" faced controversy due to its lyrics and imagery, including an image of activist Malcolm X alongside the offensive term "nigga," which sparked debate about racial and cultural sensitivities in the United States.
evidence_sentences: " Lookin Ass " ( originally titled " Lookin Ass Nigga " ) is a song by American rapper and singer Nicki Minaj .
On March 11 , 2014 , " Lookin Ass " was serviced to urban contemporary radio in the United States as Young Money : Rise of an Empires third official single .
It was sent to US rhythmic radio stations on March 18 , 2014 , two weeks after its predecessor , " Trophies " .

title: Conrad O. Johnson
entities_description: Conrad O. Johnson was an American music educator.
evidence_sentences: Conrad Oberon Johnson ( November 15 , 1915 – February 3 , 2008 ) was an American music educator , long associated with the city of Houston , who was inducted into the Texas Bandmasters Hall of Fame in 2000 .
Born in Victoria , Texas , Conrad Johnson was nine when his family moved to Houston .
Johnson was a proficient musician in his own right and , at one point , played with Count Basie .

title: Conrad O. Johnson
entities_description: Conrad O. Johnson began his career in music education in 1941.
evidence_sentences: Conrad Oberon Johnson ( November 15 , 1915 – February 3 , 2008 ) was an American music educator , long associated with the city of Houston , who was inducted into the Texas Bandmasters Hall of Fame in 2000 .
Born in Victoria , Texas , Conrad Johnson was nine when his family moved to Houston .

He started his career in music education in 1941 and , following a thirty - seven - year career , retired from his position at Kashmere High School in 1978 , but continued to remain active in shaping music in Houston by conducting summer programs and in - home tutoring .

title: Conrad O. Johnson
entities_description: Conrad O. Johnson started his career in music education at Kashmere High School and retired there, but continued to shape music in Houston by conducting programs at the school. He is also remembered through the Conrad O. Johnson School of Fine Arts at Kashmere High School.
evidence_sentences: Conrad Oberon Johnson ( November 15 , 1915 – February 3 , 2008 ) was an American music educator , long associated with the city of Houston , who was inducted into the Texas Bandmasters Hall of Fame in 2000 .
Born in Victoria , Texas , Conrad Johnson was nine when his family moved to Houston .

He started his career in music education in 1941 and , following a thirty - seven - year career , retired from his position at Kashmere High School in 1978 , but continued to remain active in shaping music in Houston by conducting summer programs and in - home tutoring .

title: Conrad O. Johnson
entities_description: Conrad Oberon Johnson was associated with Kashmere High School for a long time, both as a music educator and through the formation of the Kashmere Stage Band. He also served as the principal of the school.

title: Conrad O. Johnson
entities_description: Conrad O. Johnson was a long-time music educator associated with Kashmere High School, where he started his career in music education in 1941 and retired from in 1978, continuing to conduct summer programs and in-home tutoring. He formed the Kashmere Stage Band there which won numerous awards during its decade-long run, and the Conrad O. Johnson School of Fine Arts at Kashmere High School is named after him.
